title: Should RL replace or augment classical optimization for real-time network flow control?
subtitle: A five-lens Council Mode run with live retrieval, source-mapped claims, explicit contradictions, and deterministic verification.
audience: Senior network engineers and engineering managers at a mid-to-large ISP or cloud provider

status.headline: Live retrieval performed; production-replacement evidence remains insufficient.
status.detail: Semantic Scholar Graph API, Crossref, direct PDFs, OR-Tools, and RFC Editor were retrieved in this run. Publication identity was partly checked through DOI/source retrieval, not through the optional metadata adapter.

bottom_line: Do not replace classical LP/MIP/CP-SAT-style TE optimization with an RL-primary controller on the retrieved evidence. Use RL only as bounded augmentation: proposer, ranker, warm-start, or acceleration layer, while deterministic optimization, constraints, observability, and fallback remain the production authority.

strongest_findings.text: The strongest retrieved RL evidence is hybrid or learning-accelerated rather than RL-primary.

supported_by.note: Teal and CFR-RL both retain optimization components.

strongest_findings.title: The strongest retrieved RL evidence is hybrid or

strongest_findings.text: Production TE precedents emphasize fallback and safe updates.

supported_by.note: B4 and SWAN are the core production/production-trace evidence.

strongest_findings.title: Production TE precedents emphasize fallback and safe

strongest_findings.text: Safety constraints and risky exploration must be explicit for RL in SLA-bearing systems.

supported_by.note: Safe RL survey plus TE requirements.

strongest_findings.title: Safety constraints and risky exploration must be explicit

strongest_findings.text: No retrieved source in the required queries establishes production RL-primary TE authority.

supported_by.note: Run-scoped null evidence; not a global impossibility proof.

strongest_findings.title: No retrieved source in the required queries establishes

lens_snapshot.title: Lens posture snapshot
lens_snapshot.summary: All lenses reject immediate replacement; academic and economist lenses keep a bounded hybrid path open.
lens_snapshot.scale_label: 0 low posture intensity · 1 high posture intensity

lenses.name: practitioner
lenses.note: Requires ECMP/shortest-path fallback and shadow mode.

lenses.name: academic
lenses.note: Teal and CFR-RL support learning-accelerated designs.

lenses.name: skeptic
lenses.note: Production RL-primary evidence was not retrieved.

lenses.name: economist
lenses.note: Runtime gains need local cost evidence.

lenses.name: historian
lenses.note: Network-control innovations tend to survive as constrained layers.

contradictions.stake: Solver runtime bottleneck versus engineering cost
Classical TE precedent versus ML acceleration frontier
Production deployment evidence for RL-primary TE

options.name: A · Stay with LP/MIP/CP-SAT
options.when: Current solver meets control-loop deadlines or risk tolerance dominates.

options.name: B · Hybrid RL augmentation
points: Requires replay, shadow mode, and deterministic certification.
options.when: Solver runtime or operator workload is a real bottleneck.

options.name: C · RL-primary with LP fallback circuit
points: Production and audit evidence is insufficient.
options.when: Only after verified shadow-mode, failure-injection, and rollback evidence.

next_actions.text: Keep deterministic solvers as the system of record for production TE.
Profile local solve latency and define the control-loop deadline before funding RL.
Build a replay and failure-injection benchmark using local traffic matrices.
Run RL first in shadow mode as a proposer whose outputs are solver-certified.
Require auditable decision logs and rollback drills before any traffic exposure.

gaps.text: No production RL-primary TE case study with outage, rollback, and SLA evidence was retrieved.
No local cost model for ML infrastructure, solver latency, and on-call burden exists in this run.
No failure-injection comparison of solver-only, hybrid, and RL-primary TE was retrieved.

sources.title: Teal: Learning-Accelerated Optimization of WAN Traffic Engineering
sources.note: Direct benchmark for learning-accelerated WAN traffic engineering on Microsoft WAN traces.

sources.title: CFR-RL: Traffic Engineering With Reinforcement Learning in SDN
sources.note: RL-based critical-flow selection combined with LP rerouting for SDN traffic engineering.

sources.title: B4: Experience with a Globally-Deployed Software Defined WAN
sources.note: Production WAN TE precedent with centralized control and explicit fallback design.

sources.title: SWAN: Achieving High Utilization in Networks
sources.note: Shows operational constraints for centrally controlled inter-datacenter TE updates.

sources.title: A Comprehensive Survey on Safe Reinforcement Learning
sources.note: Frames safety constraints, risky exploration, and risk-sensitive criteria for RL controllers.

sources.title: Minimum Cost Flows
sources.note: Authoritative for classical minimum-cost-flow solver framing.

sources.title: Requirements for Traffic Engineering Over MPLS
sources.note: Defines TE objectives and MPLS TE requirements for large backbones.

sources.title: On Deep Reinforcement Learning for Traffic Engineering in SD-WAN
sources.note: Shows peer-reviewed DRL-for-SD-WAN literature exists, but this run does not use it for direct empirical support.

sources.title: Live Semantic Scholar query log for Storm Council network-flow RL run
sources.note: Records mandatory live retrieval attempts and null results for production/RL replacement queries.

minor: Open contradictions remain for human review: X-002, X-004, X-006, X-008
Contradictions marked resolved without an evidence/move basis (not counted as handled): X-003, X-005
